description_long: This is a medium-sized coniferous tree with a dense, triangular canopy composed of dark green, needle-like foliage. The branches radiate outward from a central trunk, creating a full and bushy appearance with minimal gaps between the leaves. The trunk is straight, sturdy, and dark brown, tapering slightly as it rises into the canopy. The foliage is thickest near the middle and upper sections, giving the tree a classic evergreen silhouette. The base of the trunk is clearly visible, but no roots are exposed above ground. This tree would be commonly found in urban parks, along streets, or in landscaped areas, providing shade and greenery.
description: A dense, dark green coniferous tree with a triangular canopy and a sturdy, straight trunk. The needle-like foliage forms a full, bushy silhouette typical of evergreens.
description_view_0: Front view shows the triangular, symmetrical canopy and the straight, dark trunk at the center.
description_view_1: Side view reveals the depth of the canopy and the outward spread of the branches, with the trunk partially obscured by foliage.
description_view_2: Rear view highlights the dense foliage, with the trunk mostly hidden and the canopy appearing widest.
description_view_3: Opposite side view displays the thick, overlapping branches and the trunk visible at the base, similar to the front but with slight asymmetry in branch distribution.
